title: Pest Control Products Act

provisions.heading: Short Title
provisions.text: This Act may be cited as the Pest Control Products Act.

provisions.label: “active ingredient”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: active ingredient means a component of a pest control product to which the intended effects of the product are attributed and includes a synergist but does not include a solvent, diluent, emulsifier or other component that is not primarily responsible for those effects. (principe actif)

provisions.label: “advertise”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: advertise includes making a representation by any means for the purpose of directly or indirectly promoting the distribution of a pest control product. (publicité)

provisions.label: “analyst”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: analyst means an individual who is appointed or designated as an analyst under section 45. (analyste)

provisions.label: “biological diversity”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: biological diversity means the variability among living organisms from all sources, including, without limiting the generality of the foregoing, terrestrial and marine and other aquatic ecosystems and the ecological complexes of which they form a part and includes the diversity within and between species and of ecosystems. (diversité biologique)

provisions.label: “biotechnology”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: biotechnology means the application of science and engineering in the direct or indirect use of living organisms or parts or products of living organisms in their natural or modified forms. (biotechnologie)

provisions.label: 2(1)“conditions of registration”(a)
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: any conditions specified by the Minister under paragraph 8(1)(a) or subsection 8(2) or when amending the registration of a pest control product under this Act; and

provisions.label: 2(1)“conditions of registration”(b)
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: any other requirements stated by this Act or the regulations to be conditions of registration. (conditions d’homologation)

provisions.label: “conditions of registration”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: conditions of registration means

provisions.label: “confidential business information”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: confidential business information means information to which access may be refused under the Access to Information Act and that meets the requirements of subsection 43(4) or (5). (renseignements commerciaux confidentiels)

provisions.label: “confidential test data”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: confidential test data means test data to which access may be refused under the Access to Information Act. (données d’essai confidentielles)

provisions.label: “distribute”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: distribute means distribute in any way, whether or not for consideration, and includes sell, offer for sale or distribution, and expose, display or advertise for sale or distribution. (distribution)

provisions.label: “document”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: document means anything on which information that is capable of being understood by a person, or read by a computer or other device, is recorded or marked. (document)

provisions.label: “ecosystem”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: ecosystem means a dynamic complex of plant, animal and micro-organism communities and their non-living environment interacting as a functional unit. (écosystème)

provisions.label: 2(1)“environment”(a)
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: air, land and water;

provisions.label: 2(1)“environment”(b)
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: all layers of the atmosphere;

provisions.label: 2(1)“environment”(c)
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: all organic and inorganic matter and living organisms; and

provisions.label: 2(1)“environment”(d)
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: the interacting natural systems that include components referred to in paragraphs (a) to (c). (environnement)

provisions.label: “environment”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: environment means the components of the Earth and includes

provisions.label: “environmental risk”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: environmental risk, in respect of a pest control product, means the possibility of harm to the environment, including its biological diversity, resulting from exposure to or use of the product, taking into account its conditions or proposed conditions of registration. (risque environnemental)

provisions.label: “formulant”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: formulant means any component of a pest control product that is added intentionally to the product and that is not an active ingredient. (formulant)

provisions.label: “government policy”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: government policy means the Toxic Substances Management Policy issued by the Government of Canada in June, 1995, as long as it remains in effect, and any other policies of the Government of Canada that are prescribed. (politique gouvernementale)

provisions.label: “health risk”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: health risk, in respect of a pest control product, means the possibility of harm to human health resulting from exposure to or use of the product, taking into account its conditions or proposed conditions of registration. (risque sanitaire)

provisions.label: “inspector”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: inspector means an individual who is appointed or designated as an inspector under section 45. (inspecteur)

provisions.label: 2(1)“label”(a)
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: that is or is to be applied or attached to or included in, or that accompanies or is to accompany, a pest control product or a package; or

provisions.label: 2(1)“label”(b)
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: that belongs to a pest control product and is transmitted electronically, in accordance with the regulations. (étiquette)

provisions.label: “label”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: label includes any written, printed or graphic matter

provisions.label: “manufacture”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: manufacture includes produce, formulate, package, label and prepare for distribution or use. (fabrication)

provisions.label: “Minister”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: Minister means the Minister of Health. (ministre)

provisions.label: “package”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: package includes a container, wrapping, covering or holder in which a pest control product is wholly or partly contained, placed or packed. (emballage)

provisions.label: “penalty”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: penalty means an administrative monetary penalty imposed under the Agriculture and Agri-Food Administrative Monetary Penalties Act for a violation. (pénalité)

provisions.label: “person”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: person means Her Majesty, an individual or an organization as defined in section 2 of the Criminal Code. (personne)

provisions.label: “pest”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: pest means an animal, a plant or other organism that is injurious, noxious or troublesome, whether directly or indirectly, and an injurious, noxious or troublesome condition or organic function of an animal, a plant or other organism. (parasite)

provisions.label: 2(1)“pest control product”(a)
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: a product, an organism or a substance, including a product, an organism or a substance derived through biotechnology, that consists of its active ingredient, formulants and contaminants, and that is manufactured, represented, distributed or used as a means for directly or indirectly controlling, destroying, attracting or repelling a pest or for mitigating or preventing its injurious, noxious or troublesome effects;

provisions.label: 2(1)“pest control product”(b)
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: an active ingredient that is used to manufacture anything described in paragraph (a); or

provisions.label: 2(1)“pest control product”(c)
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: any other thing that is prescribed to be a pest control product. (produit antiparasitaire)

provisions.label: “pest control product”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: pest control product means

provisions.label: “place”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: place includes a means of transport. (lieu)

provisions.label: “prescribed”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: prescribed means prescribed by regulation. (Version anglaise seulement)

provisions.label: “Register”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: Register means the Register of Pest Control Products established and maintained under section 42. (Registre)

provisions.label: “registrant”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: registrant means a person in whose name a pest control product is registered. (titulaire)

provisions.label: “test data”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: test data means scientific or technical information respecting the health or environmental risks or the value of a pest control product. (données d’essai)

provisions.label: “threshold effect”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: threshold effect, in respect of a pest control product, means a harmful effect on human health for which the Minister is able to identify a level at which the product will not cause that effect. (effet de seuil)

provisions.label: 2(1)“value”(a)
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: efficacy;

provisions.label: 2(1)“value”(b)
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: effect on host organisms in connection with which it is intended to be used; and

provisions.label: 2(1)“value”(c)
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: health, safety and environmental benefits and social and economic impact. (valeur)

provisions.label: “value”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: value, in respect of a pest control product, means the product’s actual or potential contribution to pest management, taking into account its conditions or proposed conditions of registration, and includes the product’s

provisions.label: 2(1)“violation”(a)
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: any contravention of any provision of this Act or of a regulation made under this Act; and

provisions.label: 2(1)“violation”(b)
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: any refusal or neglect to perform any duty imposed by or under this Act. (violation)

provisions.label: “violation”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: violation means any of the following that may be proceeded with in accordance with the Agriculture and Agri-Food Administrative Monetary Penalties Act:

provisions.label: “workplace”
provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: workplace means a place where an individual works for remuneration. (lieu de travail)

provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: The definitions in this subsection apply in this Act.

provisions.heading: Interpretation
provisions.text: For the purposes of this Act, the health or environmental risks of a pest control product are acceptable if there is reasonable certainty that no harm to human health, future generations or the environment will result from exposure to or use of the product, taking into account its conditions or proposed conditions of registration.

provisions.heading: Her Majesty
provisions.text: This Act is binding on Her Majesty in right of Canada or a province.

provisions.heading: Mandate
provisions.text: In the administration of this Act, the Minister’s primary objective is to prevent unacceptable risks to individuals and the environment from the use of pest control products.

provisions.label: 4(2)(a)
provisions.heading: Mandate
provisions.text: support sustainable development designed to enable the needs of the present to be met without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs;

provisions.label: 4(2)(b)
provisions.heading: Mandate
provisions.text: seek to minimize health and environmental risks posed by pest control products and encourage the development and implementation of innovative, sustainable pest management strategies by facilitating access to pest control products that pose lower risks and by other appropriate measures;

provisions.label: 4(2)(c)
provisions.heading: Mandate
provisions.text: encourage public awareness in relation to pest control products by informing the public, facilitating public access to relevant information and public participation in the decision-making process; and

provisions.label: 4(2)(d)
provisions.heading: Mandate
provisions.text: ensure that only those pest control products that are determined to be of acceptable value are approved for use in Canada.

provisions.heading: Mandate
provisions.text: Consistent with, and in furtherance of, the primary objective, the Minister shall

provisions.heading: Mandate
provisions.text: For greater certainty, protection and consideration afforded to children in this Act shall also extend to future generations.

provisions.heading: Advisory Council
provisions.text: In carrying out duties under this Act, the Minister may establish an advisory council of persons whose interests and concerns are affected by this Act, and may specify the functions of the council and the means by which it is to perform those functions.

provisions.heading: Advisory Council
provisions.text: The advisory council shall give any report that it makes, including its recommendations and the reasons for them, to the Minister, who shall place it in the Register.

provisions.heading: Prohibitions
provisions.text: No person shall manufacture, possess, handle, store, transport, import, distribute or use a pest control product that is not registered under this Act, except as otherwise authorized under subsection 21(5) or 41(1), section 48 or 51, any of sections 53 to 59 or the regulations.

provisions.heading: Prohibitions
provisions.text: Except as otherwise authorized under section 53.3 or 54, no person shall manufacture, import, export or distribute a registered pest control product unless it conforms to the conditions of registration respecting its composition and the person complies with the other conditions of registration.

provisions.heading: Prohibitions
provisions.text: Except as otherwise authorized under section 53, 53.3 or 54, no person shall store, import, export or distribute a pest control product that is not packaged and labelled in accordance with the regulations and, if it is registered, the conditions of registration.

provisions.heading: Prohibitions
provisions.text: [Repealed, 2016, c. 9, s. 35]

provisions.label: 6(5)(a)
provisions.heading: Prohibitions
provisions.text: the regulations; or

provisions.label: 6(5)(b)
provisions.heading: Prohibitions
provisions.text: if the product is registered, the directions on the label recorded in the Register, subject to the regulations.

provisions.heading: Prohibitions
provisions.text: No person shall handle, store, transport, use or dispose of a pest control product in a way that is inconsistent with

provisions.heading: Prohibitions
provisions.text: [Repealed, 2016, c. 9, s. 35]

provisions.heading: Prohibitions
provisions.text: No person shall package, label or advertise a pest control product in a way that is false, misleading or likely to create an erroneous impression regarding its character, value, quantity, composition, safety or registration.

provisions.heading: Prohibitions
provisions.text: No person shall manufacture, possess, handle, store, transport, distribute, use or dispose of a pest control product in a way that endangers human health or safety or the environment.

provisions.heading: Prohibitions
provisions.marginalNote: Alteration, destruction or falsification — required documents
provisions.text: No person shall alter, destroy or falsify a document that they are required under this Act to keep, maintain or provide.

provisions.label: 6(8.2)(a)
provisions.heading: Prohibitions
provisions.text: alter a document issued or made — or in any manner given — under this Act; or

provisions.label: 6(8.2)(b)
provisions.heading: Prohibitions
provisions.text: have in their possession, or use, a document issued or made — or in any manner given — under this Act that has been altered.

provisions.heading: Prohibitions
provisions.marginalNote: Alteration, possession or use — official documents
provisions.text: It is prohibited for a person to

provisions.heading: Prohibitions
provisions.marginalNote: Possession or use of documents that resemble official documents
provisions.text: It is prohibited for a person to have in their possession, or use, any document that has not been issued or made — or in any manner given — under this Act if the document so resembles a document issued or made — or in any manner given — under this Act that it is likely to be mistaken for such a document.

provisions.label: 6(9)(a)
provisions.heading: Prohibitions
provisions.text: on summary conviction, to a fine of not more than $200,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than six months, or to both; or

provisions.label: 6(9)(b)
provisions.heading: Prohibitions
provisions.text: on conviction on indictment, to a fine of not more than $500,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than three years, or to both.

provisions.heading: Prohibitions
provisions.text: A person who contravenes any provision of this section is guilty of an offence and liable

provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.text: An application to register a pest control product or to amend the product’s registration must be made to the Minister in the form and manner directed by the Minister and must include any information or other thing that is required by the regulations to accompany the application.

provisions.label: 7(2)(a)
provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.text: is relevant to the registered pest control product that contains the equivalent active ingredient; and

provisions.label: 7(2)(b)
provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.text: is necessary to support the application.

provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.marginalNote: Use of information provided by registrants
provisions.text: If the Minister determines that the active ingredient of the applicant’s pest control product is equivalent to the active ingredient of a registered pest control product, the Minister shall, subject to and in accordance with the regulations, permit the applicant to use or rely on any information referred to in subsection (1) that has been provided by any registrant if the Minister is satisfied that the information

provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.text: For the purposes of subsection (1), the applicant may include information that is available from a review or evaluation of a pest control product conducted by the government of another member country of the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development if the proposed use of the pest control product in Canada would be under conditions similar to those under which the foreign review or evaluation was conducted.

provisions.label: 7(3)(a)
provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.text: in accordance with the regulations, if any, conduct any evaluations that the Minister considers necessary with respect to the health or environmental risks or the value of the pest control product;

provisions.label: 7(3)(b)
provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.text: expedite evaluations with respect to a pest control product that may reasonably be expected to pose lower health or environmental risks; and

provisions.label: 7(3)(c)
provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.text: carry out any consultation required by section 28.

provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.text: If the Minister is satisfied that the application has been made in accordance with subsection (1), (2) or (2.1), the Minister shall

provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.text: The Minister may, by delivering a notice in writing, request an applicant to provide the Minister with other information in support of the application within the time and in the form specified in the notice.

provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.text: The Minister shall deny an application if the applicant does not comply with a notice under subsection (4).

provisions.label: 7(6)(a)
provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.text: the applicant has the burden of persuading the Minister that the health and environmental risks and the value of the pest control product are acceptable; and

provisions.label: 7(6)(b)
provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.text: the Minister shall consider the information provided by the applicant in support of the application and may consider additional information, but the Minister shall give the applicant a reasonable opportunity to make representations in respect of the additional information before completing the evaluation.

provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.marginalNote: Burden of persuasion and consideration of information
provisions.text: During an evaluation,

provisions.label: 7(7)(a)
provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.text: apply a scientifically based approach; and

provisions.label: 7(7)(b)(i)
provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.text: among other relevant factors, consider available information on aggregate exposure to the pest control product, namely dietary exposure and exposure from other non-occupational sources, including drinking water and use in and around homes and schools, and cumulative effects of the pest control product and other pest control products that have a common mechanism of toxicity,

provisions.label: 7(7)(b)(ii)
provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.text: apply appropriate margins of safety to take into account, among other relevant factors, the use of animal experimentation data and the different sensitivities to pest control products of major identifiable subgroups, including pregnant women, infants, children, women and seniors, and

provisions.label: 7(7)(b)(iii)
provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.text: in the case of a threshold effect, if the product is proposed for use in or around homes or schools, apply a margin of safety that is ten times greater than the margin of safety that would otherwise be applicable under subparagraph (ii) in respect of that threshold effect, to take into account potential pre- and post-natal toxicity and completeness of the data with respect to the exposure of, and toxicity to, infants and children unless, on the basis of reliable scientific data, the Minister has determined that a different margin of safety would be appropriate.

provisions.label: 7(7)(b)
provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.text: in relation to health risks, if a decision referred to in paragraph 28(1)(a) or (b) is being made or has been made in relation to a pest control product,

provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.text: In evaluating the health and environmental risks of a pest control product and in determining whether those risks are acceptable, the Minister shall

provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.marginalNote: Government policy to be given effect in evaluation
provisions.text: In evaluating the health and environmental risks and the value of a pest control product, the Minister shall give effect to government policy.

provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.text: In determining whether the health and environmental risks and the value of a pest control product are acceptable, the Minister may, in accordance with the regulations, if any, take into account information regarding the risks and value of other pest control products that are registered for the same use.

provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.text: For the purposes of subsection (9), the Minister shall, before making the determination, give the applicant a reasonable opportunity to make representations in respect of the information referred to in that subsection.

provisions.label: 8(1)(a)
provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.text: specifying the conditions relating to its manufacture, handling, storage, transport, import, export, packaging, distribution, use or disposition, including conditions relating to its composition, and, subject to subsection (2), the conditions relating to its label;

provisions.label: 8(1)(b)
provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.text: assigning a registration number to the product in the case of a new registration and, where the Minister considers it appropriate, in the case of an amendment; and

provisions.label: 8(1)(c)
provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.text: specifying the period for which the registration or amended registration is valid, which period may be either finite or indefinite.

provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.text: If the Minister considers that the health and environmental risks and the value of the pest control product are acceptable after any required evaluations and consultations have been completed, the Minister shall register the product or amend its registration in accordance with the regulations, if any, by

provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.text: The Minister may specify conditions relating to the label of a pest control product, otherwise than in accordance with the regulations, if the Minister is satisfied that the purposes of this Act can be met by so doing.

provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.marginalNote: Provision of safety information to workplaces
provisions.text: Without limiting the generality of paragraph (1)(a), the Minister shall specify, as a condition of registration, the requirement for product safety information, including a material safety data sheet for the product, to be provided to workplaces where the pest control product is used or manufactured, in accordance with the regulations made under paragraph 67(1)(s).

provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.text: The Minister shall deny an application referred to in subsection 7(1) if the Minister does not consider that the health or environmental risks of a pest control product are, or its value is, acceptable.

provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.text: A registrant of a pest control product shall, as a condition of registration, record, retain and report to the Minister information on sales of the product in the form and manner directed by the Minister and in accordance with the regulations made under paragraph 67(1)(u).

provisions.heading: Applications for Registration or Amendment
provisions.text: The obligation under subsection (5) to retain and report sales information in respect of a pest control product continues to apply to a former registrant after that product ceases to be registered.

provisions.heading: Maximum Residue Limits
provisions.marginalNote: Specification at time of registration decision
provisions.text: When making a decision regarding the registration of a pest control product, the Minister shall, if necessary, specify any maximum residue limits for the product or for its components or derivatives that the Minister considers appropriate in the circumstances.

provisions.heading: Maximum Residue Limits
provisions.text: The Minister may specify maximum residue limits for an unregistered pest control product or its components or derivatives, or for a registered pest control product or its components or derivatives with respect to a use that is not provided for by its registration, whether or not an application under subsection (2) is made for that purpose.

provisions.heading: Maximum Residue Limits
provisions.text: Any person may make an application to the Minister to specify maximum residue limits pursuant to subsection (1). Section 7, with any necessary modifications, applies to that application.

provisions.heading: Maximum Residue Limits
provisions.text: When specifying maximum residue limits for a pest control product or its components or derivatives pursuant to subsection (1), the Minister shall evaluate only the health risks of the product or its components or derivatives.

provisions.heading: Maximum Residue Limits
provisions.text: The health risks associated with maximum residue limits specified by the Minister under sections 9 and 10 must be considered to be acceptable by the Minister.

provisions.label: 11(2)(a)(i)
provisions.heading: Maximum Residue Limits
provisions.text: aggregate exposure to the pest control product, namely dietary exposure and exposure from other non-occupational sources, including drinking water and use in and around homes and schools,

provisions.label: 11(2)(a)(ii)
provisions.heading: Maximum Residue Limits
provisions.text: cumulative effects of the pest control product and other pest control products that have a common mechanism of toxicity, and

provisions.label: 11(2)(a)(iii)
provisions.heading: Maximum Residue Limits
provisions.text: the different sensitivities to pest control products of major identifiable subgroups, including pregnant women, infants, children, women and seniors; and

provisions.label: 11(2)(a)
provisions.heading: Maximum Residue Limits
provisions.text: among other relevant factors, consider available information on

provisions.label: 11(2)(b)
provisions.heading: Maximum Residue Limits
provisions.text: in the case of a threshold effect, apply a margin of safety that is ten times greater than the margin of safety that would otherwise be applicable under subparagraph 7(7)(b)(ii) or 19(2)(b)(ii) in respect of that threshold effect, to take into account potential pre- and post-natal toxicity and completeness of the data with respect to the exposure of, and toxicity to, infants and children, unless, on the basis of reliable scientific data, the Minister has determined that a different margin of safety would be appropriate.

provisions.heading: Maximum Residue Limits
provisions.text: If a decision referred to in paragraph 28(1)(a) or (b) is being made or has been made in relation to a pest control product, the Minister shall, in evaluating and determining whether the health risks associated with maximum residue limits for that pest control product or its components or derivatives are acceptable under subsection (1),

provisions.label: 12(1)(a)
provisions.heading: Additional Information and Mandatory Reporting
provisions.text: to compile information, conduct tests and monitor experience with the pest control product for the purpose of obtaining additional information with respect to its effects on human health and safety or the environment or with respect to its value; and

provisions.label: 12(1)(b)
provisions.heading: Additional Information and Mandatory Reporting
provisions.text: to report the additional information to the Minister within the time and in the form specified in the notice.

provisions.heading: Additional Information and Mandatory Reporting
provisions.text: The Minister may, by delivering a notice in writing, require a registrant

provisions.heading: Additional Information and Mandatory Reporting
provisions.text: A requirement under subsection (1) is a condition of registration.

provisions.heading: Additional Information and Mandatory Reporting
provisions.text: An applicant for registration of a pest control product, a person who makes an application under subsection 10(2) or a registrant shall report any prescribed information that relates to the health or environmental risks or the value of the pest control product to the Minister within the prescribed time and in the form and manner directed by the Minister.

provisions.heading: Additional Information and Mandatory Reporting
provisions.text: After considering any information reported under section 12 or 13, the Minister shall determine whether a special review of the registration of the pest control product should be initiated.

provisions.label: 15(a)
provisions.heading: Additional Information and Mandatory Reporting
provisions.text: the pest control product poses a significant health or environmental risk; or

provisions.label: 15(b)
provisions.heading: Additional Information and Mandatory Reporting
provisions.text: it is in the public interest to do so.

provisions.heading: Additional Information and Mandatory Reporting
provisions.text: After considering any information reported under section 13, the Minister shall place his or her conclusions in the Register and shall make public those conclusions if, in the Minister’s opinion,

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: The Minister may initiate the re-evaluation of a registered pest control product if the Minister considers that, since the product was registered, there has been a change in the information required, or the procedures used, for the evaluation of the health or environmental risks or the value of pest control products of the same class or kind.

provisions.label: 16(2)(a)
provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: if a decision of a type referred to in paragraph 28(1)(a) or (b) was made in relation to a pest control product on or after April 1, 1995, the Minister shall initiate a re-evaluation of that product no later than one year after 15 years have elapsed since the most recent decision of that type; and

provisions.label: 16(2)(b)
provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: if the most recent decision of a type referred to in paragraph 28(1)(a) or (b) was made in relation to a pest control product before April 1, 1995, the Minister shall initiate a re-evaluation of that product no later than April 1, 2005 or the date that is one year after 15 years have elapsed since that decision, whichever date is later.

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: Without limiting the generality of subsection (1),

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: Re-evaluation of a pest control product is initiated by the Minister delivering a notice in writing to the registrant explaining the reasons for initiating the re-evaluation and, if considered necessary by the Minister, requiring the registrant to provide information in the form and within the period specified in the notice.

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.marginalNote: Request for information from departments and provinces
provisions.text: After the re-evaluation is initiated, the Minister shall deliver a notice to federal and provincial government departments and agencies whose interests and concerns are affected by the federal regulatory system requesting them to provide, in the form and within the period specified in the notice, information in respect of the health and environmental risks and the value of the product that is under re-evaluation.

provisions.label: 16(5)(a)
provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: two or more registrants may provide the information required under subsection (3) or paragraph 19(1)(a) jointly; and

provisions.label: 16(5)(b)
provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: if the Minister is satisfied that the information required under subsection (3) or paragraph 19(1)(a) has been provided by one or more registrants, the Minister shall, subject to and in accordance with the regulations, permit another registrant to use or rely on that information to meet the requirements under that subsection or paragraph.

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.marginalNote: Provision of information if more than one registrant
provisions.text: If there is more than one registrant whose registered pest control products have active ingredients that the Minister has determined to be equivalent,

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: If the active ingredients of the registered pest control product that is subject to the re-evaluation are not equivalent to the active ingredients in another registrant’s registered pest control product, the Minister shall, subject to and in accordance with the regulations, permit the registrant whose product is subject to the re-evaluation to use or rely on information provided by the other registrant if the Minister is satisfied that the information is necessary for the re-evaluation.

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: After the re-evaluation is initiated, the Minister shall, in accordance with the regulations, if any, conduct any evaluations that the Minister considers necessary with respect to the health or environmental risks or the value of the pest control product and shall carry out the consultations required by section 28.

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: The Minister shall initiate a special review of the registration of a pest control product if the Minister has reasonable grounds to believe that the health or environmental risks of the product are, or its value is, unacceptable.

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: Without limiting the generality of subsection (1), when a member country of the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development prohibits all uses of an active ingredient for health or environmental reasons, the Minister shall initiate a special review of registered pest control products containing that active ingredient.

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.marginalNote: Special review where information from department or province
provisions.text: Without limiting the generality of subsection (1), the Minister shall initiate a special review of the registration of a pest control product if a federal or provincial government department or agency has provided information to the Minister that relates to the health or environmental risks or the value of the product and if, after considering the information provided, the Minister has reasonable grounds to believe that the health or environmental risks of the product are, or its value is, unacceptable.

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: Any person may request a special review of the registration of a pest control product by making a request to the Minister in the form and manner directed by the Minister.

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: Within a reasonable time after receiving a request, the Minister shall decide whether to initiate a special review and shall respond to the request with written reasons for the decision.

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: For the purposes of this section, the Minister shall initiate a special review only in relation to the aspect of the pest control product that prompted the special review.

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: If the Minister has initiated a re-evaluation of, or a special review in relation to, a pest control product, the Minister may, at any time before the decision statement is made public under subsection 28(5), expand the scope of the re-evaluation or special review to include any aspect of the product that would otherwise prompt a new special review under subsection (1), (2) or (3).

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: If the Minister expands the scope of a re-evaluation or special review under subsection (7) after the consultation statement relating to the re-evaluation or special review has been made public under subsection 28(2), the Minister shall make public a new or amended consultation statement under that subsection that takes into account the aspect referred to in subsection (7).

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: Despite section 17, the Minister may decide not to initiate a special review in relation to a pest control product if a re-evaluation of, or a special review in relation to, the product has already been initiated that includes the aspect of the product that would otherwise prompt a special review.

provisions.label: 17.1(2)(a)
provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: the Minister made public under subsection 28(5) a decision statement respecting a re-evaluation of, or a special review in relation to, that product;

provisions.label: 17.1(2)(b)
provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: the aspect of the product that would otherwise prompt a special review was addressed by the re-evaluation or special review referred to in paragraph (a); and

provisions.label: 17.1(2)(c)
provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: the Minister determines that there is no additional information in relation to the health or environmental risks of the product that provides the Minister with reasonable grounds to believe that those risks are unacceptable.

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.marginalNote: Discretion of Minister — previous decision statement
provisions.text: Despite subsection 17(2), the Minister may decide not to initiate a special review of a registered pest control product under that subsection if

provisions.label: 17.2(a)
provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: a decision made under subsection 17(7) to expand the scope of a re-evaluation or special review to include an aspect that would otherwise prompt a new special review under subsection 17(2);

provisions.label: 17.2(b)
provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: a decision made under subsection 17.1(1) or (2) not to initiate a special review in relation to an aspect that would otherwise prompt such a review under subsection 17(2).

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: The Minister shall make public each of the following decisions and the reasons for it:

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: A special review of a pest control product is initiated by the Minister delivering a notice in writing to the registrant explaining the reasons for initiating the special review and, if considered necessary by the Minister, requiring the registrant to provide information in the form and within the period specified in the notice.

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.marginalNote: Request for information from departments and provinces
provisions.text: After the special review is initiated, the Minister shall deliver a notice to federal and provincial government departments and agencies whose interests and concerns are affected by the federal regulatory system requesting them to provide, in the form and within the period specified in the notice, information in respect of the health and environmental risks and the value of the product that is under special review.

provisions.label: 18(3)(a)
provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: two or more registrants may provide the information required under subsection (1) or paragraph 19(1)(a) jointly; and

provisions.label: 18(3)(b)
provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: if the Minister is satisfied that the information required under subsection (1) or paragraph 19(1)(a) has been provided by one or more registrants, the Minister shall, subject to and in accordance with the regulations, permit another registrant to use or rely on that information to meet the requirements under that subsection or paragraph.

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.marginalNote: Provision of information if more than one registrant
provisions.text: If there is more than one registrant whose registered pest control products have active ingredients that the Minister has determined to be equivalent,

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: If the active ingredients of a registered pest control product that is subject to the special review are not equivalent to the active ingredients in another registrant’s registered pest control product, the Minister shall, subject to and in accordance with the regulations, permit the registrant whose product is subject to the special review to use or rely on information provided by the other registrant if the Minister is satisfied that the information is necessary for the special review.

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: After the special review is initiated, the Minister shall, in accordance with the regulations, if any, evaluate only the aspects of the pest control product that are within the scope of the special review and shall carry out the consultations required by section 28.

provisions.label: 19(1)(a)
provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: the Minister may, by delivering a notice in writing, require the registrant to provide, in the form and within the period specified in the notice, additional information that the Minister considers necessary for the evaluation;

provisions.label: 19(1)(b)
provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: the registrant has the burden of persuading the Minister that the health and environmental risks and the value of the pest control product are acceptable; and

provisions.label: 19(1)(c)
provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: the Minister shall consider the information provided by the registrant in support of the product and may consider any additional information, but the Minister shall give the registrant a reasonable opportunity to make representations in respect of the additional information before completing the evaluation.

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: During an evaluation that is done in the course of a re-evaluation or special review,

provisions.label: 19(2)(a)
provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: apply a scientifically based approach; and

provisions.label: 19(2)(b)(i)
provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: among other relevant factors, consider available information on aggregate exposure to the pest control product, namely dietary exposure and exposure from other non-occupational sources, including drinking water and use in and around homes and schools, and cumulative effects of the pest control product and other pest control products that have a common mechanism of toxicity,

provisions.label: 19(2)(b)(ii)
provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: apply appropriate margins of safety to take into account, among other relevant factors, the use of animal experimentation data and the different sensitivities to pest control products of major identifiable subgroups, including pregnant women, infants, children, women and seniors, and

provisions.label: 19(2)(b)(iii)
provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: in the case of a threshold effect, if the product is used in or around homes or schools, apply a margin of safety that is ten times greater than the margin of safety that would otherwise be applicable under subparagraph (ii) in respect of that threshold effect, to take into account potential pre- and post-natal toxicity and completeness of the data with respect to the exposure of, and toxicity to, infants and children, unless, on the basis of reliable scientific data, the Minister has determined that a different margin of safety would be appropriate.

provisions.label: 19(2)(b)
provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: in relation to health risks,

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: In evaluating the health and environmental risks of a pest control product and in determining whether those risks are acceptable, the Minister shall

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.marginalNote: Government policy to be given effect in evaluation
provisions.text: In evaluating the health and environmental risks and the value of a pest control product, the Minister shall give effect to government policy.

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: In determining whether the health and environmental risks and the value of a pest control product are acceptable, the Minister may, in accordance with the regulations, if any, take into account information regarding the risks and value of other pest control products that are registered for the same use.

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: For the purposes of subsection (4), the Minister shall, before making the determination, give any registrant whose product is under re-evaluation or special review a reasonable opportunity to make representations in respect of the information referred to in that subsection.

provisions.label: 20(1)(a)
provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: the registrant fails to satisfy a requirement under subsection 16(3) or 18(1) or paragraph 19(1)(a); or

provisions.label: 20(1)(b)
provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: in the course of a re-evaluation or special review, the Minister has reasonable grounds to believe that the cancellation or amendment is necessary to deal with a situation that endangers human health or safety or the environment, taking into account the precautionary principle set out in subsection (2).

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: The Minister may cancel or amend the registration of a pest control product if

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: Where there are threats of serious or irreversible damage, lack of full scientific certainty shall not be used as a reason for postponing cost-effective measures to prevent adverse health impact or environmental degradation.

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: The Minister may rescind any action taken under subsection (1) if the circumstances that prompted it cease to exist.

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: If the registration of a pest control product is cancelled or amended under paragraph (1)(a), the Minister may, for a prescribed period, refuse to consider any application made in respect of that product by the registrant.

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: If the Minister considers that the health and environmental risks and the value of a pest control product are acceptable after any required evaluations and consultations have been completed, the Minister shall confirm the registration.

provisions.label: 21(2)(a)
provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: amend the registration if the Minister considers that the health and environmental risks and value of the product would be acceptable after the amendment; or

provisions.label: 21(2)(b)
provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: cancel the registration.

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: If the Minister does not consider that the health or environmental risks or value of a pest control product are acceptable, the Minister shall

provisions.label: 21(3)(a)
provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: no suitable alternative to the use of the pest control product is available; and

provisions.label: 21(3)(b)
provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: the Minister considers that the health and environmental risks and value of the product are acceptable until the effective date of the amendment or cancellation.

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: The Minister may delay the effective date of the amendment or cancellation if

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: A delay is subject to any conditions that the Minister considers necessary for carrying out the purposes of this Act.

provisions.label: 21(5)(a)
provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: allow the continued possession, handling, storage, distribution and use of stocks of the product in Canada at the time of cancellation, subject to any conditions, including disposal procedures, that the Minister considers necessary for carrying out the purposes of this Act;

provisions.label: 21(5)(b)
provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: require the registrant to recall and dispose of the product in a manner specified by the Minister; or

provisions.label: 21(5)(c)
provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.text: seize and dispose of the product.

provisions.heading: Re-evaluation and Special Review
provisions.marginalNote: Continued possession, etc., of existing stocks
provisions.text: When cancelling the registration of a pest control product under this section or any other provision of this Act, the Minister may

provisions.heading: Other Grounds for Cancellation or Amendment
provisions.text: A registrant who intends to discontinue the sale of a pest control product for one or more uses for which it is registered shall notify the Minister of that intention in the form and manner directed by the Minister.

provisions.heading: Other Grounds for Cancellation or Amendment
provisions.text: The Minister may deliver a notice in writing to the registrant requiring the registrant to explain the reasons for the discontinuation.

provisions.heading: Other Grounds for Cancellation or Amendment
provisions.text: On receipt of notification under subsection (1), the Minister shall cancel or amend the registration, as the case may be, as of a date to be determined by the Minister and, pending that date, may impose any conditions that the Minister considers necessary for carrying out the purposes of this Act.

provisions.label: 23(1)(a)
provisions.heading: Other Grounds for Cancellation or Amendment
provisions.text: cancel or amend any registration in the registrant’s name; and

provisions.label: 23(1)(b)
provisions.heading: Other Grounds for Cancellation or Amendment
provisions.text: refuse to consider any application made by the registrant under this Act.

provisions.heading: Other Grounds for Cancellation or Amendment
provisions.text: If a registrant fails to pay a fee, fine, penalty, charge or cost that the registrant is liable to pay under or in relation to this Act, the Minister may

provisions.heading: Other Grounds for Cancellation or Amendment
provisions.text: Before taking any action under subsection (1) in relation to charges or costs, the Minister shall give the registrant a reasonable opportunity to make representations.

provisions.heading: Other Grounds for Cancellation or Amendment
provisions.text: The Minister shall immediately give written notice to the registrant of any action taken under subsection (1) and of the reasons for the action.

provisions.heading: Other Grounds for Cancellation or Amendment
provisions.text: The Minister may, with the written consent of the registrant, amend the registration of a pest control product for the purpose of reducing its health or environmental risks or increasing its value.

provisions.heading: Other Grounds for Cancellation or Amendment
provisions.text: The Minister may cancel or amend the registration of a pest control product if the registrant does not comply with the conditions of registration.

provisions.label: 26(a)
provisions.heading: Other Grounds for Cancellation or Amendment
provisions.text: cancel or amend the registration of the pest control product that was involved in the violation or offence where the person who committed the violation or offence is the registrant;

provisions.label: 26(b)
provisions.heading: Other Grounds for Cancellation or Amendment
provisions.text: cancel or amend the registration of any other pest control product in respect of which the person is the registrant; or

provisions.label: 26(c)
provisions.heading: Other Grounds for Cancellation or Amendment
provisions.text: refuse to consider any application made under this Act by the person during any period that the Minister considers appropriate.

provisions.heading: Other Grounds for Cancellation or Amendment
provisions.text: If a person is found to have committed a violation or is convicted of an offence under this Act, the Minister may, having regard to the nature of the violation or offence and the circumstances surrounding its commission,

provisions.heading: Other Grounds for Cancellation or Amendment
provisions.text: The Governor in Council may, by order, cancel or amend the registration of a pest control product or a class of pest control products if the Governor in Council considers it necessary to do so to implement an international agreement.

provisions.label: 28(1)(a)(i)
provisions.heading: Public Consultation
provisions.text: to register a pest control product that is or contains an unregistered active ingredient, or

provisions.label: 28(1)(a)(ii)
provisions.heading: Public Consultation
provisions.text: to register, or amend the registration of, a pest control product if the Minister considers that registration or amendment of the registration may result in significantly increased health or environmental risks;

provisions.label: 28(1)(a)
provisions.heading: Public Consultation
provisions.text: to grant or deny an application

provisions.label: 28(1)(b)
provisions.heading: Public Consultation
provisions.text: about the registration of a pest control product on completion of a re-evaluation or special review; or

provisions.label: 28(1)(c)
provisions.heading: Public Consultation
provisions.text: about any other matter if the Minister considers it in the public interest to do so.

provisions.heading: Public Consultation
provisions.text: The Minister shall consult the public and federal and provincial government departments and agencies whose interests and concerns are affected by the federal regulatory system before making a decision

provisions.heading: Public Consultation
provisions.text: To initiate a consultation under subsection (1), the Minister shall make public a consultation statement and shall invite any person to send written comments on the proposed decision within the period specified in the statement.

provisions.label: 28(3)(a)
provisions.heading: Public Consultation
provisions.text: a summary of any reports of the evaluation of the health and environmental risks and the value of the pest control product prepared or considered by the Minister;

provisions.label: 28(3)(b)
provisions.heading: Public Consultation
provisions.text: the proposed decision and the reasons for it; and

provisions.label: 28(3)(c)
provisions.heading: Public Consultation
provisions.text: any other information that the Minister considers necessary in the public interest.

provisions.heading: Public Consultation
provisions.text: The consultation statement shall include

provisions.heading: Public Consultation
provisions.text: The Minister shall consider any comments received pursuant to subsection (2) before making a decision.

provisions.heading: Public Consultation
provisions.text: After making a decision, the Minister shall make public a decision statement that shall include the decision, the reasons for it and a summary of any comments that the Minister received on the proposed decision.

provisions.heading: Public Consultation
provisions.text: A consultation statement referred to in subsection (2) and a decision statement referred to in subsection (5) shall contain any confidential test data that the Minister considers to be in the public interest.

provisions.heading: Offences
provisions.text: Every person who does not comply with section 13 is guilty of an offence.

provisions.heading: Offences
provisions.text: A registrant who does not comply with a requirement in a notice referred to in subsection 16(3) or 18(1), paragraph 19(1)(a) or subsection 22(2) is guilty of an offence.

provisions.label: 30(1)(a)
provisions.heading: Offences
provisions.text: about the health or environmental risks or the value of a pest control product; or

provisions.label: 30(1)(b)
provisions.heading: Offences
provisions.text: in response to a notice delivered under this Act.

provisions.heading: Offences
provisions.text: Every person is guilty of an offence if they knowingly provide the Minister with false or misleading information

provisions.heading: Offences
provisions.text: Every person is guilty of an offence if they falsely claim to have conducted a test relating to the health or environmental risks or the value of a pest control product, knowingly conduct such a test that is misleading or knowingly provide false or misleading information about such a test.

provisions.heading: Offences
provisions.text: A registrant who does not comply with the conditions of registration is guilty of an offence.

provisions.heading: Offences
provisions.marginalNote: Non-compliance with other conditions and requirements
provisions.text: A person who does not comply with a condition imposed under subsection 21(4), paragraph 21(5)(a) or subsection 22(3) or with a requirement under paragraph 21(5)(b) is guilty of an offence.

provisions.label: 32(a)
provisions.heading: Offences
provisions.text: on summary conviction, to a fine of not more than $200,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than six months, or to both; or

provisions.label: 32(b)
provisions.heading: Offences
provisions.text: on conviction on indictment, to a fine of not more than $500,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than three years, or to both.

provisions.heading: Offences
provisions.text: Every person who commits an offence under any of sections 29 to 31 is liable

provisions.heading: Export Controls
provisions.text: The Governor in Council may, by order, establish a Pest Control Products Export Control List consisting of pest control products that meet the prescribed criteria.

provisions.heading: Export Controls
provisions.text: No person shall export a pest control product that is on the Pest Control Products Export Control List, except as authorized under this Act.

provisions.heading: Export Controls
provisions.text: An application for authorization to export a pest control product must be made to the Minister in the form and manner directed by the Minister.

provisions.label: 33(4)(a)
provisions.heading: Export Controls
provisions.text: the applicant satisfies the Minister that the prescribed requirements for the authorization are or will be met; and

provisions.label: 33(4)(b)
provisions.heading: Export Controls
provisions.text: the proposed export is not prohibited under any other Act of Parliament.

provisions.heading: Export Controls
provisions.text: The Minister may authorize an applicant to export a pest control product to a specified country if

provisions.heading: Export Controls
provisions.text: The authorization is subject to any conditions that the Minister considers necessary for carrying out the purposes of this Act.

provisions.heading: Export Controls
provisions.text: The Minister shall give public notice of the authorization.

provisions.label: 33(7)(a)
provisions.heading: Export Controls
provisions.text: on summary conviction, to a fine of not more than $200,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than six months, or to both; or

provisions.label: 33(7)(b)
provisions.heading: Export Controls
provisions.text: on conviction on indictment, to a fine of not more than $500,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than three years, or to both.

provisions.heading: Export Controls
provisions.text: Every person who contravenes subsection (2) is guilty of an offence and liable

provisions.label: 33(8)(a)
provisions.heading: Export Controls
provisions.text: on summary conviction, to a fine of not more than $200,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than six months, or to both; or

provisions.label: 33(8)(b)
provisions.heading: Export Controls
provisions.text: on conviction on indictment, to a fine of not more than $500,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than three years, or to both.

provisions.heading: Export Controls
provisions.marginalNote: Offence and punishment — false or misleading information
provisions.text: Every person who knowingly provides false or misleading information in connection with an authorization is guilty of an offence and liable

provisions.label: 34(1)(a)
provisions.heading: Export Controls
provisions.text: has reasonable grounds to believe that a prescribed requirement of the authorization is not or will not be met;

provisions.label: 34(1)(b)
provisions.heading: Export Controls
provisions.text: has reasonable grounds to believe that the conditions to which the authorization is subject have not been or will not be met; or

provisions.label: 34(1)(c)
provisions.heading: Export Controls
provisions.text: becomes aware of additional information about the health or environmental risks of the pest control product.

provisions.heading: Export Controls
provisions.text: The Minister may amend, suspend or cancel an authorization to export a pest control product if the Minister

provisions.heading: Export Controls
provisions.text: Before amending or cancelling an authorization or after suspending one, the Minister shall give the authorized person a reasonable opportunity to make representations.

provisions.heading: Export Controls
provisions.text: After considering any representations that may be made by the authorized person, the Minister shall reinstate, amend or cancel the authorization.

provisions.heading: Export Controls
provisions.text: The Minister shall give public notice of the amendment or cancellation of an authorization.

provisions.heading: Reconsideration of Decisions
provisions.text: Any person may file with the Minister, in the form and manner directed by the Minister, a notice of objection to a decision referred to in paragraph 28(1)(a) or (b) within 60 days after the decision statement referred to in subsection 28(5) is made public.

provisions.heading: Reconsideration of Decisions
provisions.marginalNote: Notice of objection to authorization decisions
provisions.text: Any person may file with the Minister, in the form and manner directed by the Minister, a notice of objection to a decision to authorize the export of a pest control product or to amend or cancel an authorization within 60 days after a notice referred to in subsection 33(6) or 34(4) is made public.

provisions.heading: Reconsideration of Decisions
provisions.text: After receiving a notice of objection, the Minister may, in accordance with the regulations, if any, establish a panel of one or more individuals to review the decision and to recommend whether the decision should be confirmed, reversed or varied.

provisions.heading: Reconsideration of Decisions
provisions.text: The Minister shall give public notice of the establishment of a review panel.

provisions.heading: Reconsideration of Decisions
provisions.marginalNote: Reasons to be provided if panel not established
provisions.text: If the Minister does not establish a panel, the Minister shall provide written reasons without delay to the person who filed the notice of objection.

provisions.heading: Reconsideration of Decisions
provisions.text: The Minister may determine the terms of reference of a review panel and the procedure for the review, and may at any time change them.

provisions.heading: Reconsideration of Decisions
provisions.text: A review panel shall give any person a reasonable opportunity to make representations in respect of the decision under review, in accordance with the terms of reference.

provisions.heading: Reconsideration of Decisions
provisions.text: Subject to subsections 44(3) and (6), the hearings of a review panel shall be open to the public.

provisions.heading: Reconsideration of Decisions
provisions.marginalNote: Information to be placed in Register
provisions.text: A review panel shall give the information submitted to it to the Minister, who shall place it in the Register.

provisions.heading: Reconsideration of Decisions
provisions.text: The filing of a notice of objection or the establishment of a review panel does not suspend the decision under review, but the Minister may suspend the decision until a final decision is made on completion of the review or until the review panel is dissolved.

provisions.heading: Reconsideration of Decisions
provisions.text: The Minister may dissolve a review panel if all notices of objection are withdrawn by the persons who filed them.

provisions.heading: Reconsideration of Decisions
provisions.text: As soon as possible after the conclusion of a review, the review panel shall submit to the Minister a report stating its recommendations and the reasons for them.

provisions.heading: Reconsideration of Decisions
provisions.text: The Minister shall place the review panel’s report in the Register.

provisions.heading: Reconsideration of Decisions
provisions.text: After considering the recommendations of a review panel, the Minister shall confirm, reverse or vary the decision under review, but the confirmation, reversal or variation must not result in the registration, either initial or continued, of a pest control product unless the Minister considers that its health and environmental risks and its value are acceptable.

provisions.heading: Reconsideration of Decisions

provisions.heading: Authorization to Use Unregistered Product
provisions.text: The Minister may, in accordance with the regulations and subject to any conditions that the Minister may specify, authorize a person to use an unregistered pest control product for a specified purpose.

provisions.heading: Authorization to Use Unregistered Product
provisions.text: The Minister shall authorize the use of a pest control product if the Minister considers that use of the product for the specified purpose in accordance with any conditions specified does not pose unacceptable health or environmental risks.

provisions.heading: Authorization to Use Unregistered Product
provisions.text: The Minister shall suspend an authorization if the Minister has reasonable grounds to believe that the conditions to which the authorization is subject have not been or will not be met or that the continuance of the authorization poses unacceptable health or environmental risks.

provisions.heading: Authorization to Use Unregistered Product
provisions.text: After suspending an authorization, the Minister shall give the authorized person a reasonable opportunity to make representations.

provisions.heading: Authorization to Use Unregistered Product
provisions.text: After considering any representations that may be made by the authorized person, the Minister shall reinstate, amend or cancel the authorization.

provisions.heading: Authorization to Use Unregistered Product

provisions.text: if the pest control product or other thing is perishable, order its owner or the person having possession, care or control of it at the time of its seizure to dispose of it at the expense of the person being so ordered or, on notice to its owner or the person having possession, care or control of it at the time of its seizure, dispose of it at the expense of the person to whom the notice is given; or

provisions.text: if the inspector is of the opinion that the pest control product or other thing endangers human health or safety or the environment and that its disposition is necessary to respond to the danger, order its owner or the person having possession, care or control of it at the time of its seizure to dispose of it, despite subsection 6(8), at the expense of the person being so ordered or, on notice to its owner or the person having possession, care or control of it at the time of its seizure, dispose of it, despite subsection 6(8), at the expense of the person to whom the notice is given.

provisions.heading: Dealing with Seized Things

provisions.heading: Dealing with Seized Things
provisions.text: If an inspector is satisfied that the provisions of this Act and the regulations that apply with respect to a pest control product or other thing seized under this Act have been complied with, the product or thing must be released.

provisions.heading: Dealing with Seized Things
provisions.text: Subject to subsection 55(1), if proceedings are instituted in relation to a pest control product or other thing seized under this Act, its owner or the person having possession, care or control of it at the time of its seizure may apply, in the case of a violation, to the Review Tribunal continued by subsection 27(1) of the Agriculture and Agri-Food Administrative Monetary Penalties Act or, in the case of an offence, to the court before which the proceedings are being held, for an order that the thing be returned.

provisions.heading: Dealing with Seized Things
provisions.text: If the Review Tribunal or court, as the case may be, is satisfied that sufficient evidence exists or may reasonably be obtained without the continued detention of the pest control product or other thing, the Tribunal or court may order it to be returned to the applicant, subject to any conditions the Tribunal or court may impose to ensure that it is preserved for any purpose for which it may subsequently be required.

provisions.heading: Removal, Forfeiture or Destruction of Unlawful Imports
provisions.text: An inspector who has reasonable grounds to believe that an imported pest control product does not meet the requirements of the regulations or was imported in contravention of a provision of this Act or the regulations may decide whether to give the owner or importer, or the person having possession, care or control of the product, the opportunity to take a measure in respect of it.

provisions.label: 53.3(2)(a)
provisions.heading: Removal, Forfeiture or Destruction of Unlawful Imports
provisions.text: whether the pest control product endangers human health or safety or the environment; and

provisions.label: 53.3(2)(b)
provisions.heading: Removal, Forfeiture or Destruction of Unlawful Imports
provisions.text: any other prescribed factors.

provisions.heading: Removal, Forfeiture or Destruction of Unlawful Imports
provisions.text: In making a decision under subsection (1), the inspector shall consider, among other factors

provisions.heading: Removal, Forfeiture or Destruction of Unlawful Imports
provisions.text: If the inspector decides under subsection (1) not to give the owner or importer or the person having possession, care or control of the pest control product the opportunity to take a measure in respect of it, the inspector shall exercise, in respect of the product, any of the powers conferred by the provisions of this Act, other than this section, or of the regulations.

provisions.heading: Removal, Forfeiture or Destruction of Unlawful Imports
provisions.marginalNote: Measures that may be taken and notice
provisions.text: However, if the inspector decides under subsection (1) to give the owner or importer, or the person having possession, care or control of the pest control product the opportunity to take a measure in respect of it, the inspector, or any other inspector who is informed of the decision, shall decide whether the owner or importer, or the person having possession, care or control of it may remove it from Canada at their expense, consent to its forfeiture or take either of these measures, and shall notify or cause to be notified the owner or importer, or the person having possession, care or control of the product that they may take that measure within the period specified by the inspector or other inspector, as the case may be.

provisions.heading: Removal, Forfeiture or Destruction of Unlawful Imports
provisions.text: If a person is notified under subsection (4) that they may consent to the forfeiture of the pest control product and the person consents to its forfeiture, the product is forfeited to Her Majesty in right of Canada and may be disposed of, as the Minister may direct, at the person’s expense.

provisions.heading: Removal, Forfeiture or Destruction of Unlawful Imports
provisions.text: An inspector who has reasonable grounds to believe that an imported pest control product does not meet the requirements of the regulations or was imported in contravention of a provision of this Act or the regulations may, by written notice, whether the pest control product is seized or not, order its owner or importer, or the person having possession, care or control of it, to remove it from Canada at their expense or, if removal is not possible, to destroy it at their expense.

provisions.heading: Removal, Forfeiture or Destruction of Unlawful Imports
provisions.text: If the pest control product is not removed from Canada, or destroyed, within the period specified in the notice — or, if no period is specified, within 90 days after the day on which the notice was delivered — it is, despite section 53.1, forfeited to Her Majesty in right of Canada and may be disposed of, as the Minister may direct, at the expense of the person to whom the notice was delivered.

provisions.label: 54(3)(a)
provisions.heading: Removal, Forfeiture or Destruction of Unlawful Imports
provisions.text: the pest control product does not endanger human health or safety or the environment;

provisions.label: 54(3)(b)
provisions.heading: Removal, Forfeiture or Destruction of Unlawful Imports
provisions.text: the pest control product will not be sold within that period;

provisions.label: 54(3)(c)
provisions.heading: Removal, Forfeiture or Destruction of Unlawful Imports
provisions.text: the measures that should have been taken for the pest control product not to have been imported in contravention of a provision of this Act or the regulations will be taken within that period; and
